title: Two arrested in Lethbridge cocaine seizure
service_name: ALERT - Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams
content:
Two people have been arrested after nearly $400,000 worth of cocaine was seized in Lethbridge.
ALERT Lethbridge’s organized crime team, with help from Lethbridge Police Service, made the seizure after searching two homes, a storage unit, and a vehicle. The search warrant executions took place on May 20, 2026.
Two suspects were arrested in the days following the seizure. The most recent arrest took place on May 28, 2026.
ALERT seized approximately $400,000 in drugs and cash:
3,929 grams of cocaine;
223 grams of methamphetamine;
Firearm ammunition;
Body armour; and
$2,775 cash.
ALERT had been investigating the pair since November 2025 based on information that they had been drug trafficking in the Lethbridge community. The May 20th search warrants coincided with a drug shipment arriving from Calgary.
“Organized crime stretches beyond municipal boundaries and does not discriminate in how it inflicts harm on communities. This case exemplifies how ALERT teams share intelligence and enforcement strategies to disrupt the drug trade,” said Staff Sergeant Trevor Sheppard, ALERT.
Two suspects have been charged with offences related to drug trafficking –
Drew Boake
, 36 years old from Lethbridge and
Griffin Walkey
, 21 years old from Lethbridge.
Boake and Walkey are scheduled to appear in court on June 11, 2026.
Members of the public who suspect drug or gang activity in their community can call local police, or contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477). Crime Stoppers is always anonymous.
ALERT was established and is funded by the Alberta Government and is a compilation of the province’s most sophisticated law enforcement resources committed to tackling serious and organized crime.
Report A Crime
The work we do is about our communities, and that’s why we recognize that residents can be instrumental in tackling serious crime. If you or someone you know has been the victim of a crime, or you suspect criminal activity, please reach out. Your information will remain strictly confidential.
Thank you for helping to make Alberta safer by being actively involved and reporting suspicious activity.

title: Extensive child sexual exploitation materials collection
service_name: ALERT - Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams
content:
A collection of over half a million child sexual exploitation photos and videos have been discovered following the arrest of an Okotoks man.
Totaling more than 500,000 child sexual exploitation photos and videos, the collection spanned several hard drives and computers seized from an Okotoks home on May 26, 2026. It’s one of the largest collections Internet Child Exploitation unit has ever encountered.
“Behind every one of the photos and videos is a real child somewhere in this world that is being exploited for a sexual purpose. A collection of this size is deeply disturbing, as it would have also likely been used to trade and fuel demand,” said Staff Sergeant Gordon MacDonald, ICE.
For context, in the past year, ICE identified 622,150 photos and videos containing child sexual exploitation province-wide from all its investigations.
The collection allegedly belonged to a 35-year-old Okotoks man
Corey Airhart
who has been charged with possessing and accessing child sexual abuse materials.
ICE’s investigation began in January 2026 when the RCMP’s National Child Exploitation Crime Centre shared information about the suspect uploading child sexual exploitation materials. Okotoks RCMP provided help with the investigation.
Airhart was released from custody and is scheduled to appear in court on August 8, 2026 in Okotoks.
Anyone with information about this case is asked to contact local police or Crime Stoppers (1-800-222-TIPS).

title: Calgary man arrested for child sexual exploitation offences
service_name: ALERT - Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams
content:
A 70-year-old Calgary man has been charged child sexual exploitation offences. ICE is concerned given the extreme nature of the offence-related content that was allegedly being shared.
Michael Penfold
was arrested by ALERT’s Internet Child Exploitation (ICE) unit on May 28, 2026 at a home in the southeast neighbourhood of Cranston. The Calgary Police Service helped with the arrest and search of the suspect’s home.
Penfold was charged with making available, possessing, and accessing child sexual exploitation materials. He had allegedly been sharing and trading photos online.
“Unquestionably all child sexual exploitation materials are offensive, but in this case the content is particularly egregious. This arrest is a small step towards curbing these photos and videos from being traded online,” said Staff Sergeant Gordon MacDonald, ALERT ICE.
Penfold was identified following a proactive initiative by ICE investigators that seeks to target online offenders trading the most extreme, graphic, and violent sexual materials involving children.
Anyone with information about this case is asked to contact local police or Crime Stoppers (1-800-222-TIPS).
Penfold remains in custody to await his next scheduled court appearance for June 18, 2026.
ALERT was established and is funded by the Alberta Government and is a compilation of the province’s most sophisticated law enforcement resources committed to tackling serious and organized crime.

title: Regina Police: One Charged in Human Trafficking Investigation
service_name: ALERT - Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams
content:
Calgary| Human Trafficking
A 28 year-old male from Calgary, AB is facing human trafficking charges following an investigation led by the Regina Police Service. The charge, and several others, were laid as part of a complex, multi-agency investigation called ‘Project Anchor’.
The investigation began in December of 2025 when Regina Police Service investigators received information about a female victim being trafficked in the sex trade. An investigation showed the victim had been trafficked throughout western Canada, including in Regina. These offences are believed to have occurred between December 2023, and November 2025.
Investigators from the Regina Police Service Vice Unit worked with the Calgary Police Service (CPS) and the Alberta Law Enforcement Response Team (ALERT) Human Trafficking Enforcement Unit to investigate, locate and arrest the suspect. On May 20, 2026, Abdoulaye GADJIKO, a resident of Calgary, AB, was arrested and charged in relation to this case. Over 30 officers with the two Alberta agencies were involved in the arrest and subsequent search of four residences, one business and a vehicle. All were located in Calgary, AB. The Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada (FINTRAC) also assisted in the investigation.
In addition to the assistance from partner agencies, multiple Regina Police Service investigative units and civilian professionals contributed to the six-month project.
As a result, 28 year-old Abdoulaye GADJIKO is charged with:
Human Trafficking [CC 279.01(1)(a)];
Material Benefit Resulting from Trafficking [CC 279.02];
Procuring [CC 286.3(1)];
Material Benefit from Sexual Services (Living off the Avails) [CC 286.2(1)];
Advertising Sexual Services [CC 286.4(a)];
Possession of Proceeds of Crime over $5,000 [CC 355]
Money Laundering [CC 462.31];and
Failure to Comply [CC 145(5)]
“Human Trafficking investigations are extremely complex. most often cross borders and require extensive collaboration between agencies ” explains Deputy Chief of Police Laurel Marshall. “First and foremost, it’s about ensuring victims are safe and helping them break free from their traffickers. I am proud of the work being done by everyone involved in ‘Project Anchor’.”
Anyone with any information that may be related to this investigation is encouraged to contact their local police service.
GADJIKO will make his first appearance on these charges in Regina Provincial Court on Wednesday, May 27, 2026 at 9:30 a.m.

title: Two handguns seized from vehicle in Calgary
service_name: ALERT - Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams
content:
Two handguns, one that had its serial number defaced and the other that previously reported as stolen, have been recovered by ALERT.
ALERT Calgary’s organized crime team made the seizure on April 16, 2026 after searching a vehicle. The Calgary Police Service also helped with the search of vehicle and two residential locations in Calgary’s northeast neighbourhood. One person was arrested.
Inside the vehicle, ALERT located two handguns, magazines, ammunition, and shell casings. The one handgun was previously reported as stolen in Manitoba.
“Gun violence is a serious public safety issue that impacts Calgary and other communities across the province. ALERT works closely with our partners at the Calgary Police Service to share intelligence and take illegal firearms off the street,” said Staff Sergeant Ryan Smart, ALERT.
The handguns and shell casings are being analyzed by Calgary Police Service Forensic Firearms Lab to determine if they were used in any other crimes.
Ikjot Sidhu
, 19 years old, was charged with the following offences:
Possession of a restricted firearm;
Possession of a prohibited device;
Unauthorized possession of a firearm in a vehicle;
Carrying a concealed weapon;
Possession of a firearm with ammunition;
Possession of a firearm obtained by crime;
Possession of a firearm with an altered serial number; and
Careless use of a firearm.
Sidhu was released from custody and is scheduled to appear in court on May 29, 2026.
Sidhu’s arrest came as part of an organized crime investigation based on information received regarding firearms activity.
ALERT was established and is funded by the Alberta Government and is a compilation of the province’s most sophisticated law enforcement resources committed to tackling serious and organized crime.

title: Defense lawyer arrested for child luring
service_name: ALERT - Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams
content:
A Calgary criminal defense attorney is facing numerous child sexual exploitation offences, including child luring, following an ALERT investigation.
Jordan Bonner
, 46 years old, was arrested on April 30, 2026 by ALERT’s Internet Child Exploitation (ICE) unit along with the Calgary Police Service. Bonner’s Calgary home and law office were searched.
ALERT alleges that Bonner was engaged in sexually explicit online conversations with multiple youth and that he also shared child sexual exploitation materials.
Bonner used Snapchat to communicate with his alleged victims, who would have been between 11 and 15 years old. He used the online aliases
“jrcbonner
”, “
whydoyoucare197
”, and “
daddysboi10
”.
ICE is looking to identify potential victims and is encouraging anyone with information about this case to come forward and contact police. Anyone with information is encouraged to contact local police or Crime Stoppers (1-800-222-TIPS).
The investigation remains ongoing as ICE is completing a forensic analysis on computers and electronic devices that were seized.
The alleged offences took place in December 2025, according to an investigative referral shared with ALERT by the RCMP’s National Center for Missing and Exploited Children.
Bonner has been charged with:
Child luring;
Making child sexual abuse materials available to a child;
Making child sexual abuse materials;
Distributing child sexual abuse materials;
Possessing child sexual abuse materials;
Accessing child sexual abuse materials;
Unauthorized possession of a firearm;
Possession of a firearm knowing its possession is unauthorized; and
Careless storage of a firearm.
Bonner was released from custody to await a scheduled court appearance on May 7, 2026.
ALERT was established and is funded by the Alberta Government and is a compilation of the province’s most sophisticated law enforcement resources committed to tackling serious and organized crime.
Report A Crime
The work we do is about our communities, and that’s why we recognize that residents can be instrumental in tackling serious crime. If you or someone you know has been the victim of a crime, or you suspect criminal activity, please reach out. Your information will remain strictly confidential.
Thank you for helping to make Alberta safer by being actively involved and reporting suspicious activity.

title: Handguns, cocaine seized in Fort McMurray
service_name: ALERT - Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams
content:
General
Fort McMurray…
Two handguns and over two kilograms of cocaine, worth nearly a quarter million dollars’ in street value, were seized after a series of homes were searched in Fort McMurray.
ALERT Fort McMurray’s organized crime team made the seizure on April 13, 2026 when three homes and two vehicles were searched. Wood Buffalo RCMP and Fort McMurray Fire Department helped with the search warrant executions.
“ALERT, in partnership with the Wood Buffalo RCMP, remain committed to ensuring the safety of the members of the community.  Any time we can get drug dealers into custody and take firearms off the streets, that is a great outcome,” said Staff Sergeant Mark Wait, ALERT.
The drugs seized have an approximate street value of $242,000, which includes over two kilograms of cocaine and varying amounts of illicit prescription pills. ALERT also seized $5,320 in cash and one of the vehicles is being held as offence related property.
ALERT also seized two handguns and nearly 100 rounds of ammunition. Neither of the firearms were lawfully possessed and given previous release conditions, the suspects were barred from possessing any firearms.
Six suspects were charged with a combined 35 criminal offences related to drugs and firearms:
Mohamed Bare
, 26 years old;
Hassan Ahmed
, 22 years old;
Musap Ahmed
, 29 years old;
Yusef Ali
, 38 years old;
Yoonis Ibrahim
, 26 years old; and
Ridwan Dalmar
, 26 years old.
Ibrahim and Dalmar’s whereabouts are unknown at this time and warrants have been issued for their arrests.
The remaining suspects remain in custody.
The investigation began in March 2026 when ALERT received information about suspected drug trafficking in the region.
Members of the public who suspect drug or gang activity in their community can call local police, or contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477). Crime Stoppers is always anonymous.
ALERT was established and is funded by the Alberta Government and is a compilation of the province’s most sophisticated law enforcement resources committed to tackling serious and organized crime.
Report A Crime
The work we do is about our communities, and that’s why we recognize that residents can be instrumental in tackling serious crime. If you or someone you know has been the victim of a crime, or you suspect criminal activity, please reach out. Your information will remain strictly confidential.
Thank you for helping to make Alberta safer by being actively involved and reporting suspicious activity.

title: Fentanyl, other drugs seized in Lloydminster traffic stop
service_name: ALERT - Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams
content:
A large amount of fentanyl, cocaine, and methamphetamine were seized following an ALERT investigation in Lloydminster.
ALERT Lloydminster’s organized crime team, with the assistance of Lloydminster RCMP, made the drug seizure on April 16, 2026. The drugs were found in vehicle after a traffic stop was conducted just outside of the city limits.
“Given the recent fentanyl overdoses experienced in Lloydminster, this is a large amount of fentanyl to remove from circulation and hopefully break some of the addiction cycle,” said Insp. Brad Lundeen, ALERT.
“This success shows to highlight the strength of what is achievable when ALERT and the RCMP come together with a shared commitment, protecting and strengthening our community in Lloydminster,” said Insp. Brian Nicholl, Lloydminster RCMP.
The seized drugs have an estimated street value of $155,000 and included:
1,000 grams of methamphetamine;
527 grams of fentanyl;
503 grams of cocaine; and
5 litres of GHB.
One person was arrested but charges have yet to been laid.
ALERT’s investigation remains ongoing.
Members of the public who suspect drug or gang activity in their community can call local police, or contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477). Crime Stoppers is always anonymous.
ALERT was established and is funded by the Alberta Government and is a compilation of the province’s most sophisticated law enforcement resources committed to tackling serious and organized crime.

title: Edmonton man faces charges of human trafficking, bestiality
service_name: ALERT - Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams
content:
Numerous criminal charges have been laid against an Edmonton man for allegedly sexually exploiting and human trafficking at least five women.
Jonathan Allard
has been charged with 26 criminal code offence and two Animal Protection Act charges following a lengthy investigation by ALERT Edmonton’s Human Trafficking unit. The 47-year-old was arrested on March 26, 2026 with help from the Edmonton Police Service.
Allard is accused of human trafficking offences in the Edmonton area involving several women. ALERT has identified five victims and believes there could be more.
“We know there are at least five other victims. We would like to speak to them and anyone else who has been exploited by Allard. These are extreme circumstances with life altering impact and trauma. We would like to hold Allard accountable for all his victims,” said Staff Sergeant Chris Hayes, ALERT.
Anyone with information about this case is asked to contact local police or Crime Stoppers (1-800-222-TIPS).
The victims have been provided support resources through ALERT’s Safety Network Coordinators. Survivors of human trafficking can call 211 for help.
The investigation dates back to June 2025 when one of his alleged victims contacted police for help. She alleges she was recruited via social media to move to Edmonton and was then forced into the sex trade by Allard.
ALERT’s investigation identified other victims of Allard’s and allege that he was involved in arranging sexual services, communicating with clients, collecting money, and controlling the victims.
In addition to the women, there was evidence of a dog being sexually and physically abused by Allard.
Allard has been charged with:
Trafficking in a persons;
Receive material benefit from trafficking;
Forcible confinement;
Assault;
Procuring sexual services;
Advertising sexual services of another person;
Bestiality;
Causing unnecessary pain, suffering, or injury to a dog;
Animal neglect;
Cause of permit an animal to be in distress; and
Failing to ensure an animal has adequate food or water.
Allard remains in custody to await his next court appearance scheduled for April 16, 2026.

Ghost Guns are illegal, privately manufactured firearms or lower receivers. These weapons are often made with 3D-printers, and undermine public safety due to their lack of licensing requirements, serialization and safety controls.
